mechanism_narrative: A lower federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening aggregate demand, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the threshold fails to separate positive from negative return periods with > 70% accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising retail sales and a declining federal funds rate amplifies consumer spending power while lowering financing costs, creating a compounding effect that generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative or statistically insignificant coefficient in a multivariate regression of forward returns, or the combined signal fails to outperform either proxy alone in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production indicates strengthening real economic output and higher capacity utilization, which directly supports near-term corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during late-cycle expansions, or 5-day forward returns show a negative correlation with INDPRO growth when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: A consistent month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls reflects sustained labor market expansion and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or payroll growth fails to predict positive forward returns with a p-value below 0.05 in a univariate model.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year consumer price index growth rate exceeding 3.0% triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuation multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances when the inflation threshold is breached, or the threshold fails to predict return direction with > 65% accuracy in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of high housing starts and low unemployment signals robust residential investment and strong labor market fundamentals, which together drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal shows no incremental predictive power over individual series, or the interaction term produces a negative coefficient in a multivariate forecasting model.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarterly real GDP growth confirms expanding aggregate demand and higher corporate profit margins, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power during late-cycle expansions, or 60-day forward returns show a negative correlation with GDP growth when the output gap is positive.
